examples: And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
Acts 1:23 and they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
The prospect of a United States of Europe is about as real as the bronze of Justus Lipsius’s bust.
It is an anglicised version of the Latin justus, meaning, as one might guess, "just", "fair", "equitable", "lawful", "proper".
Over lunch at the Justus Lipsius building, the 27 EU heads of government were due to hold an emergency debate on north Africa.
